design_philosophy.core_loop: Tag-team party combat where player switches between three characters in real-time to navigate dungeons, solve puzzles, and defeat enemies. Only active character can attack; others follow passively.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management through character switching combined with positioning and weapon trade-offs (sword's broad strokes vs bow's precision vs foil's balance)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as three eighth-grade girls who become Magic Knights to rescue Princess Emeraude from the world of Cephiro; experiencing their individual perspectives through character journals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects traditional RPG progression; upgrades and spells are gated by story progression rather than grinding/achievement. Weapon variety creates distinct playstyles but all three characters are required simultaneously as a tag-team mechanic rather than optional party composition.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Working Designs enhanced enemy AI and increased enemy speed in NA localization because they regarded Japanese version as too easy, suggesting some difficulty adjustment but limited player control

progression.upgrade_system: Story-gated progression model: character upgrades and magic spells acquired at predetermined story points rather than through grinding. Maximum HP and MP increased by finding special items. Weapons and armor upgraded automatically as part of general level progression.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression unlocks new abilities, spells, and improved equipment. Three unique weapons unlock their upgraded forms at specific narrative moments.
replayability_hooks: Three playable characters with distinct weapon mechanics (sword, foil, bow) encourage different playstyles
Character journals provide individual perspectives on events
New locations and characters expand beyond source anime material

world_building_techniques: Character journals that update after key events, providing internal perspectives
Anime source material adapted with game-exclusive locations and characters
Zagato's minions interact with party throughout journey (unlike anime where some survive)

notable_bosses: Zagato (primary antagonist who abducted Princess Emeraude)

faction_indicators: Three Magic Knights vs Zagato's minion hierarchy
Cephiro's Pillar system (Princess Emeraude as magical anchor)
danger_signals: High priest Zagato's abduction of the Pillar destabilizes world
Minions (Ascot, Caldina, Rafarga) actively oppose party progression
environmental_storytelling: Cephiro world design reflects threat of Zagato's control
Game-exclusive locations expand on anime world-building
Environmental hazards that don't affect inactive tag-team members
